metadata.title: Acme Health Patient Intake API — Compliant Component

components.title: compliant-intake-api
components.description: Hardened Patient Intake API for Acme Health. Workload is Lambda + DynamoDB + S3 + API Gateway in a VPC, fronted by GitHub Actions CI with Conftest policy gate, Cosign-signed evidence bundles, and Object Lock vault. Closes all 8 gaps from the cgep-app-starter GAPS.md keyed to SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria; 5 of 8 also enforced detectively via Rego policy in CI.
components.purpose: Demonstrate end-to-end GRC engineering for a PHI workload: preventative controls in Terraform, detective controls in Rego, chain-of-custody in CI, machine-readable assurance in OSCAL.

control-implementations.description: Implementation of NIST 800-53 Rev 5 controls satisfied by the hardened Patient Intake API. Each implemented-requirement also carries a soc2-cc prop mapping to the AICPA Trust Services Criterion. Evidence URIs reference the signed pipeline bundle in the GOVERNANCE-locked evidence vault.

implemented-requirements.description: GAP-01 + GAP-02 closure. S3 uploads bucket and DynamoDB intake table are both encrypted at rest using a customer-managed KMS CMK (alias/acme-health-phi). Key rotation enabled annually. Key policy separates administrative permissions (CI role) from operational use (S3/DynamoDB service principals with confused-deputy guards via aws:SourceAccount). Detective enforcement via two Rego policies: sc28_s3_cmk.rego and sc28_ddb_cmk.rego. Surfaced as a real engineering lesson during Day 9 apply: DynamoDB calls kms:Decrypt as the caller (Lambda role), not as the service, so the Lambda role required explicit KMS permissions in both the role's inline policy and the CMK's key policy.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle (run #3, commit 6d98ee0). Verify with cosign verify-blob.

implemented-requirements.description: GAP-05 closure. Lambda function deployed inside the customer VPC across two private subnets with no internet egress. AWS data plane access (S3 + DynamoDB) via free VPC gateway endpoints associated with the main route table. NAT Gateway deliberately omitted ($32/mo saved; no security benefit). Lambda security group permits HTTPS egress only, no ingress. Detective enforcement via cc66_lambda_vpc.rego. Lesson surfaced during Day 9: gateway endpoints don't auto-associate with the main route table; explicit route_table_ids required, otherwise endpoints exist but route nothing.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle including Lambda vpc_config and gateway endpoint configuration in plan.json.

implemented-requirements.description: GAP-03 closure. All three S3 buckets (uploads, evidence vault, CloudTrail logs) deny non-TLS access via explicit aws_s3_bucket_policy with Deny + aws:SecureTransport = false condition. Detective enforcement via sc7_tls_only.rego, which parses the bucket policy JSON and verifies the Deny statement is present. The Rego policy handles both inline jsonencode policies and data-source-driven policies (the latter accepted as valid intent when unresolved at plan time).

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle showing bucket policies in plan.json.

implemented-requirements.description: GAP-07 closure. Lambda execution role's inline policy reduced from dynamodb:* and s3:* to specific actions (dynamodb:PutItem on the intake table, s3:PutObject on uploads/* prefix) matching what handler.py actually calls. Additionally, kms:Decrypt + kms:GenerateDataKey* on the PHI CMK (required because DynamoDB calls KMS as the caller). Detective enforcement via cc63_iam_least_priv.rego which rejects any service-wide wildcard (Action = '*' or 'service:*') on Allow statements while permitting action-prefix patterns (e.g., 's3:Get*') for legitimate breadth.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle showing the narrowed IAM policy in plan.json.

implemented-requirements.description: GAP-04 closure. S3 uploads bucket has versioning enabled (aws_s3_bucket_versioning.uploads), allowing recovery from accidental or malicious overwrite of PHI submissions. Versioning also enables future Object Lock if PHI immutability becomes a requirement. Combined with GAP-01 (encrypted) and GAP-03 (TLS-only) and GAP-07 (tight IAM), the uploads bucket is recoverable-by-default for PHI ingestion. Terraform-only closure; no Rego policy because the pattern is repetitive and one example in the suite (the Object Lock + versioning on the evidence vault) makes the pattern.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle showing versioning configuration in plan.json.

implemented-requirements.description: GAP-06 closure. Lambda function has dead-letter queue (KMS-encrypted SQS), X-Ray Active tracing, and reserved concurrency intent (set to -1 unreserved-pool in sandbox account with 10-total budget; production would set to 10 leaving 990 unreserved). Failed async invocations land in the DLQ for 14 days; X-Ray traces every invocation for audit and debugging. The reserved_concurrent_executions decision documents the environment-dependent nature of the control: the GAP is closed when the control's INTENT is expressed (capacity reservation), not when a specific number is chosen.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle showing DLQ, X-Ray, and concurrency configuration.

implemented-requirements.description: GAP-08 closure (partial). API Gateway HTTP API stage has access logging enabled to a KMS-encrypted CloudWatch log group (30-day retention), with JSON-formatted log records capturing requestId, sourceIp, userAgent, method, path, status, latency, and integration errors. Stage also has throttling (100 req/s rate, 50 req/s burst) for capacity control. WAF deliberately deferred as documented future work. Terraform-only closure.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle showing API Gateway logging and throttling configuration.

implemented-requirements.description: Account-level audit log retention. Multi-region CloudTrail with log file validation enabled (auditor can detect tampering with the trail itself via AWS-published hourly signed digest files). Trail writes to a dedicated S3 bucket with SSE-KMS via the PHI CMK, public access blocked, versioning enabled, lifecycle deletion at 30 days (production would extend to 365+). Bucket policy scoped via aws:SourceArn to prevent confused-deputy attacks from other accounts' CloudTrails writing here.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle showing CloudTrail configuration.
